rulings.Case Title: GARY STRENG VS. KEYNOTE PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motoin To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions Set One, And Form Interrogatories No 17.1 And For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft Keynote Properties, Inc And Robert Camozzi
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, line 5. PLAINTIFF GARY STRENG's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions Set One, And Form Interrogatories No. 17.1, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defebdant Keynote Properties, Inc and Robert Camozzi. GRANTED. Defendant shall respond to Request for admissions as follows: RFA 1: Admit Ron Comella did not have the legal authority to act on behalf of Scott Dowdee in relation to Mr. Dowdee's residential tenancy at the PREMISES. a. Before Mr. Dowdee's coma; b. During Mr. Dowdee's coma; c. After Mr. Dowdee's death RFA 2: Admit Ron Comella did not have the legal authority to exclude Plaintiff from PREMISES during RELEVANT PERIOD. d. Before Mr. Dowdee's coma; e. During Mr. Dowdee's coma; f. After Mr. Dowdee's death Plaintiff should respond to corresponding Form Interrogatories 17.1. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $3,150. Defendant stipulated to provide responses on October 31, 2013. Responsive were non-responsive. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA KISSIG et al VS. ARTHUR CAYABYAB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT ARTHUR CAYABYAB, DAN CAYABYAB and RYAN CAYABYAB's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED (1) as to causes of action 2, 3, 4 and 9. These causes of action arise out of protective activity of service of purportedly defective and non-complaint notices of termination of tenancy; and (2) as to causes of action 14 and 15. These are mixed causes of action arising out of protected and non-protected activity, plaintiffs failed to show probability of prevailing on the merits on any part of these causes of action. Motion is DENIED as to causes of action 1, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 16, 17. Moving party failed to shift burden and show that these causes of action and/or harm alleged in these causes of action (See Salma) is based upon or results from protected activity. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiffs failed to show that defendants' motion is frivolous within the meaning of CCP 128.5. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT V. GIUSTI, TRUSTEE OF THE ROBERT V. VS. BRENT KIRSTEN AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND DBA DIGITAL COR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT BRENT KIRSTEN DEMURRER's TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurer to complaint based on misjoinder is OVERRULED. Complaint alleges that in addition to being sued as individual, Kirsten is being sued as "doing business as" Digital Core Production Group, Inc. entity unknown. This allegation must be take as true on demurer. Demurer is sustained as to the 1st cause of action for rescission with leave to amend to attach an executed contract, if any. Demurer is sustained as to the 3rd cause of action for violation of B&P 17200 to allege all element of this cause of action. Demurrer is overruled as to the 2nd cause of action for fraud. Complaint sufficiently alleges all elements of fraud as Kirsten, individually and dba. Moving party to prepare order. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. Moving party and its counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/REQ)
